DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Ira Sharlip M.D. and Golden Gate Urology Inc. demur to the First Amended Complaint brought by plaintiff Paul Solon. The unopposed demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

A complaint must separately number each cause of action and state which defendants it is directed against. (Rules of Court, Rule 2.112.) Plaintiff's complaint does not follow this rule; for example, paragraphs 4-19 are numbered as a second cause of action for negligence and a first cause of action for fraud. Plaintiff is directed to abide by Rule 2.112 in drafting his amendment.

With respect to the grounds pressed by defendants: Both defendants contend that Solon has failed to state facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for fraud and intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED).

With respect to defendants' argument that the gravamen of Solon's cause of action is professional negligence governed by MICRA, the court is unpersuaded. MICRA prescribes, inter alia, damages caps and a statute of limitations. It does not limit the kinds of causes of action that can be brought against health care providers based on alleged professional negligence. Because defendants do not raise a statute of limitations issue, and damages are not yet at issue, MICRA is irrelevant to this pleadings motion. Larson v. UHS of Rancho Springs, Inc. (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 336, is not to the contrary. That case applied the one-year MICRA limitations period to battery and IIED causes of action because those causes of action arose out of alleged professional negligence. (Id., at pp. 351-352.) The case does not hold that separate causes of action other than professional negligence do not lie.

Defendants' next argument is that Solon has failed to state facts sufficient to constitute either cause of action. With respect to fraud, "[t]he elements of fraud, which give rise to the tort action for deceit, are (a) misrepresentation (false representation, concealment, or nondisclosure); (b) knowledge of falsity (or 'scienter'); (c) intent to defraud, i.e., to induce reliance; (d) justifiable reliance; and (e) resulting damage." (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 638 [citation simplified].) Solon pleads that Sharlip committed fraud in failing to document and inform Solon that Sharlip tore his meatus during surgery. All of the acts that Solon characterizes as fraudulent occurred after the surgery and it is not clear from the pleading how Solon justifiably relied on any of the fraudulent acts or how he was damaged thereby. Solon has leave to amend.

Defendants also allege that Solon has failed to state facts giving rise to IIED. The elements of this cause of action are "(1) extreme and outrageous conduct by the defendant with the intention of causing, or reckless disregard of the probability of causing, emotional distress; (2) the plaintiff suffered severe emotional distress; and (3) the defendant's extreme and outrageous conduct was the actual and proximate cause of the severe emotional distress." (Crouch v. Trinity Christian Center of Santa Ana, Inc. (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 995, 1007.) It is not clear to the court that Solon pleads this cause of action at all; none of his cause of action headings mentions this cause of action. Moreover, Solon has not alleged intentional conduct by defendants with the intent to cause harm. Solon has leave to amend.

Defendants separately demur to all causes of action against Golden Gate Urology. No facts are stated giving rise to a cause of action against this defendant or stating why this defendant is liable for the conduct of the individuals whose conduct Solon faults. Solon has leave to amend.

Solon shall amend by April 24, 2026.

Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
